answer:
Waterfall.io is not mentioned in the provided search results. The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard that enables AI applications (hosts) to connect to external data sources and tools (servers) via JSON-RPC 2.0.

Servers provide three core primitives:
- Tools: Executable functions for LLMs to perform actions [1][2].
- Resources: Read-only data sources like files or database records [1][3].
- Prompts: Pre-built interaction templates [4][2].

Servers use either Stdio (local) or Streamable HTTP (remote) transports to communicate with MCP clients [5][2].

results.title: Build an MCP server - Model Context Protocol
